title: Go HTTP Upload and Download Gateways · Execution Trace: Http Request Admission
summary: A Go handler buffers multipart forms and S3 objects in memory, trusts proxy headers, writes status too early, and continues transferring after the client leaves. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http request admission increment in streaming Go file-gateway handler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply trusted authority, authentication, authorization, method, route, headers, body, multipart, rate, and concurrency admission before storage work.
Classify missing key, missing version, delete marker, denied access, archive restore state, throttling, cancellation, and partial downstream response.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Http Request Admission · Theory Http Request Admission
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 1552 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a streaming Go file handler on request admission and goroutine cleanup; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
